plan.description: The project aims at assessing the curing-induced distortions in composites and predicting the distortions through numerical models aligned with the experimental trials.
plan.title: ELADINE DMP

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

reference.label: National Institute for Aerospace Research Elie Carafoli
Evaluation of LAminate composite Distortion by an Integrated Numerical-Experimental approach
Evaluation of LAminate composite Distortion by an Integrated Numerical-Experimental approach (corda__h2020::865431)

description.description: In the aerospace industry, Liquid Resin Infusion (LRI) is gaining more and more importance as an out-of-autoclave alternative manufacturing technique to traditional pre-impregnated (prepreg) fabrics. The research in this field has been focused on understanding the cure and the process parameters of these materials, aiming to optimize the manufacturing process and reduce costs. A major problem derived from these technologies is the distortions induced by LRI process, affecting to composite parts due to non-uniform distribution of residual stresses. Such distortions can lead to non-uniform parts with shape distortions, which is a critical issue when trying to assembly with other parts due to mismatches in shape, leading to the rejection of such components. In this context, ELADINE project aims to understand and quantify the key manufacturing parameters that cause shape distortions on composite coupons (such as spring-in of curved parts) using an integrated numerical-experimental approach. The manufacturing process will be accurately monitored through Fiber Optic Sensors (FOS) and Dielectric sensors (DC) to understand how the process variables affect the distortion phenomena. The monitored data will feed a simulation tool for spring-in prediction for large integral composite wing structures. This article covers the preliminary results of cure monitoring and process parameters of thermoset composites implementing monitoring strategies for manufactured coupons by LRI.
description.label: Challenges of complex monitoring of the curing parameters in coupons for LRI manufacturing

descriptionTemplate.label: ARIADNEPlus Horizon2020
descriptionTemplate.description: This is the Argos instance of the ARIADNEPlus template (https://vast-lab.org/dmp/ariadneplus/form/)

fieldSet.title: State the purpose of the data collection/creation
fieldSet.description: Data collection is usually at the beginning stages of research data management lifecycles to set the background of what is needed (data generation), what is already there (data reuse) and how to best use it to fulfill the project’s objectives (why). Here you may add information about the scope and objectives of your data collection process.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: You may find a comparison of different data management lifecycles here: Plale, Beth & Kouper, Inna. (2017). The Centrality of Data: Data Lifecycle and Data Pipelines. 10.1016/B978-0-12-809715-1.00004-3.

options.label: observational (e.g., sensor data, data from surveys)
options.value: observational (e.g., sensor data, data from surveys)

options.label: derived or compiled (e.g., text mining, 3D models)
options.value: derived or compiled (e.g., text mining, 3D models)

options.label: reference or canonical (e.g., static, peer-reviewed data sets, likely published or curated, such as gene sequence databanks or chemical structures)
options.value: reference or canonical (e.g., static, peer-reviewed data sets, likely published or curated, such as gene sequence databanks or chemical structures)

options.label: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)
options.value: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)

options.label: Multimedia - jpg / jpeg, gif, tiff, png, mpeg, mp4, QuickTime
options.value: Multimedia - jpg / jpeg, gif, tiff, png, mpeg, mp4, QuickTime

options.label: Discipline specific formats - Flexible Image Transport System (FITS) in astronomy, Crystallographic Information File (CIF) for crystallography
options.value: Discipline specific formats - Flexible Image Transport System (FITS) in astronomy, Crystallographic Information File (CIF) for crystallography

options.label: Instrument specific formats - Olympus Confocal Microscope Data Format, Carl Zeiss
options.value: Instrument specific formats - Olympus Confocal Microscope Data Format, Carl Zeiss

fieldSet.title: Specify the types and formats of data collected/created
fieldSet.description: You can select type and format from the documents suggested by Archaeology Data Service (ADS) (http://tiny.cc/wo9lcz), or by the Data Archiving and Networked Services (KNAW­-DANS) (http://tiny.cc/xq9lcz)
fieldSet.extendedDescription: The main distinction between data types is between primary data and secondary data. Data collection may contain both primary and secondary data depending on the source where they have been derived from. Primary data is data that have been collected for the first time and have not undergone through data processing and/or analysis, yet. Secondary data is data that have been cleaned up, analysed and shared by others (published or unpublished) and they are those that are being typically reused. In addition, types of data unveil how they have been collected so they also depend on the process that led to their generation. Data may be found in different formats. Usually, outputs of specific instruments/ services/ tools where data are generated and/ or processed have a default way of exposing their outputs following specific standards. Most common distinctions between formats are proprietary and non proprietary formats, because they may impact data access in the long-term.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: For more information, have a look at: https://data.library.virginia.edu/data-management/plan/format-types/

fieldSet.title: State the expected size of the data to be archived (GB)
fieldSet.description: Express the value in Gigabytes

options.label: Single items (i.e. one page of a manuscript, one excavation report...)
options.value: Single items (i.e. one page of a manuscript, one excavation report...)

options.label: Datasets (a set of homogeneously structured data records, consisting of fields carrying data values)
options.value: Datasets (a set of homogeneously structured data records, consisting of fields carrying data values)

options.label: Collections (an aggregation of resources, a collection may include e.g. a textual document, a set of images, one or more datasets and other collections)
options.value: Collections (an aggregation of resources, a collection may include e.g. a textual document, a set of images, one or more datasets and other collections)

fieldSet.title: Specify the granularity of the data to be archived
Specify the origin of the data

options.label: Newly created content based on reused data
options.value: Newly created content based on reused data

fieldSet.title: What sort of data is it?
How will data be collected?
fieldSet.description: You can select from the list or add a new entry in the field "Other"

fieldSet.title: Specify if existing metadata is being reused
Outline the data utility: to whom will it be useful?
fieldSet.description: Specify whether the data produced and/or used in the project is usable by third parties, in particular, after the end of the project. If the reuse of some data is restricted, explain why

options.label: If third parties are involved in the project, consent forms from informants and test subjects are documented, and the documentation is electronically archived
options.value: If third parties are involved in the project, consent forms from informants and test subjects are documented, and the documentation is electronically archived

options.label: If you are collecting personal data, you must have informed consent for storing, processing or publishing the data
options.value: If you are collecting personal data, you must have informed consent for storing, processing or publishing the data

fieldSet.title: Describe any constrains concerning ethics and privacy

section.description: Hint: This section is usually associated with detailed activities of the project. If you are writing the DMP at a primary stage of the research process (i.e. first 6 months of the research project), think about the main activities that will be performed within Work Packages (WPs) and break them down so as to fit into a research data management lifecycle. Add new dataset description to describe different datasets or types of datasets that will be produced or re-used during the project.
section.title: Data Summary

section.description: In general terms, your research data should be 'FAIR', that is findable, accessible, interoperable and re-usable. These principles precede implementation choices and do not necessarily suggest any specific technology, standard, or implementation-solution. This template is not intended as a strict technical implementation of the FAIR principles, it is rather inspired by FAIR as a general concept. Achieving FAIR data relies equally on the choices you make throughout a research process, but also on providers whose services enable FAIRness of data to be met. Learn more about the FAIR principles: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4792175/ Learn how you can comply with FAIR principles: https://www.openaire.eu/how-to-make-your-data-fair
section.title: FAIR Data

fieldSet.title: Do you provide metadata for data discoverability?
fieldSet.description: Metadata is data about data and is an essential set of information describing scientific outputs, in the form of either physical or digital objects, in a machine-readable format. According to the expected use, metadata can be given different attributes. Most common type which enables discovery and identification are descriptive metadata. Descriptive metadata contain information about key aspects needed to search for and successfully find a given scientific output, e.g. by its title, author/creator, abstract, keywords. Moreover, metadata may be used for describing a service or a scientific instrument.

referenceTypeData.label: Select a vocabulary from the list

fieldSet.title: Specify standards used for metadata creation
fieldSet.description: Standardised vocabularies enable greater interoperability across systems. There are generic and discipline specific metadata standards which have been compiled so as to contain vital information that enable exchange while taking into consideration specificities of work and demands in different disciplines, even in different areas of work within a discipline.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: To enable the discovery of content, describe research data as consistently and completely as possible. Include enough information for the data to be accessed and understood later on. If possible, use an existing metadata schema which fits the type of data object or dataset you are describing (source PARTHENOS FAIR Guidelines). You can select from the list or add a new entry in the field “Other".
fieldSet.additionalInformation: You can search for discipline-specific standards and associate tools browsing the Research Data Alliance Metadata Standard Catalogue here: rd-alliance.github.io/metadata-directory/

options.label: Metadata is associated to each digital object
options.value: Metadata is associated to each digital object

options.label: Set up of quality assurance committee for metadata
options.value: Set up of quality assurance committee for metadata

fieldSet.title: Outline the method used to ensure that there is appropriate metadata available to ensure the understanding and reuse of data over time
fieldSet.description: You can select from the list or add a new entry in the field “Other"

fieldSet.title: Specify if metadata are updated once the data are archived
Do you assign a unique identifier to your data/resources?
fieldSet.description: Persistent and/or Permanent Identifiers (PIDs) uniquely identify objects, people, organisations and activities and can ensure that the scientific output is accessible even when the URL of the website has changed. PIDs can be assigned to research outputs including publications, data and software/code. PIDs can also be assigned to researchers, samples, organisations and projects. A PID may be connected to a metadata record describing an item rather than the item itself. PIDs are usually provided by data repositories and other deposit platforms. Re3data (https://www.re3data.org/) includes tags to show which platforms that it indexes assign PIDs to their content.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: A persistent identifier or PID is a long-lasting reference to a resource. Whatever resource it refers to, the primary purpose of the PID is to provide the information required to reliably identify, verify and locate it. A PID may be connected to a set of metadata describing an item rather than to the item itself.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: Learn more about PIDs and how to use them: https://www.openaire.eu/how-can-identifiers-improve-the-dissemination-of-your-research-outputs

fieldSet.title: Outline the approach towards search keywords
fieldSet.description: Indicate what search keywords will be provided to make data findable.

fieldSet.title: Describe how resources are retrieved from your repository
fieldSet.description: Global access mechanism

fieldSet.title: Specify if metadata of non-public resources can be made publicly available

labelData.label: If yes, please, list below the naming conventions that should be followed during the project

fieldSet.title: Will you use naming conventions for your data?
fieldSet.description: Naming conventions should be clearly described in order to make data understandable by other researchers that may reuse them after the project ends.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: Check out some examples of file naming best practices: http://tiny.cc/op8lcz

fieldSet.title: Which methodologies are used to make data findable?
What technologies are used to make data findable?
Do you have any approach for clear versioning?
fieldSet.description: Versioning may be a built-in functionality, subject to an instrument/service/tool that has been used for data processing and analysis which automatically produces versioned data. In other occasions, you will need to keep track and adapt to a versioning mechanism for your data. Here you should state how versioning takes place.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: Find out more about how to handle and arrange different versions: https://www.openaire.eu/raw-data-backup-and-versioning

section.description: Data are findable when described with metadata and vocabularies in a standardized way, assigned a Persistent Identifiers (PIDs) and are registered or indexed in a searchable resource
section.title: Making data findable, including provisions for metadata

labelData.label: If there are any restrictions on public accessibility, describe the nature of the restrictions (time embargoes, no access at all, restrictions to a certain group of users, sensitive data, etc.) and how access will be provided (by contact person, etc.). Are there well described conditions for access (i.e. a machine readable license)?

fieldSet.title: Specify which data will be made openly available
fieldSet.description: Describe the data made openly available.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: If there are any restrictions on public accessibility, describe the nature of the restrictions (time embargoes, no access at all, restrictions to a certain group of users, sensitive data, etc.) and how access will be provided (by contact person, etc.). Are there well described conditions for access (i.e. a machine readable license)?

fieldSet.title: Specify how the data will be made available

options.label: Standard Visualizer (e.g. browser, Acrobat Reader, Image Viewer)
options.value: Standard Visualizer (e.g. browser, Acrobat Reader, Image Viewer)

labelData.label: Please indicate the link to access documentation or open source code of the software needed to access the data (if available).

fieldSet.title: Specify which software tools are needed to access the data

referenceTypeData.label: Select the repository where your data will be uploaded

fieldSet.title: Specify where the data and associated metadata, code, and documentation will be deposited
fieldSet.description: Select the repository where your data will be uploaded.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: For more information on the processes, you can have a look at https://www.openaire.eu/rdm-handbook

fieldSet.title: Specify if the repository you will submit your data to is accredited/has a data management policy document

section.description: Not all data can be made publicly open, hence data can be FAIR but not open, or open but not FAIR or both FAIR and open. Data are accessible when uploaded in a data repository and retrieved by their PIDs. When data can not be shared openly, metadata should be provided (even when the data are no longer available). In the case of sensitive or personal data, anonymization or pseudonymization and specific access rights can be applied. Where accessing data requires the use of complementary methods or tools, such procedures should be documented.
section.title: Making data openly accessible

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers
Specify the data vocabularies you will follow to facilitate interoperability

fieldSet.title: Assess the interoperability of your data
fieldSet.description: Specify what standards you will use to facilitate interoperability. You can select from the list below or add a new entry in the field "Other"

selectData.label: If not, will you provide mapping to standard vocabularies?

fieldSet.title: Specify whether you will be using standard vocabularies for all data types present in your dataset, to allow inter­disciplinary interoperability?

section.description: Data are interoperable, meaning they can be easily understood and shared with other platforms and systems, when they are created using standard vocabularies and include references to other data and metadata.
section.title: Making data interoperable

selectData.label: Rights Statements for cultural heritage institutions or aggregation platforms

fieldSet.title: Specify how the data will be licensed to permit the widest reuse possible
fieldSet.description: There are a number of licenses that can be assigned to your research outputs. There are different licenses for software and for publications and data. For the latter, a common practice is the use of Creative Commons licenses which are machine readable.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: You may learn more about how to choose a license here: https://www.openaire.eu/how-do-i-license-my-research-data

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

options.label: Reuse is subordinated to legitimate interests of rights holders and protection of confidentiality and personal information
options.value: Reuse is subordinated to legitimate interests of rights holders and protection of confidentiality and personal information

options.label: Embargo date can only be handled when the technical framework allows it
options.value: Embargo date can only be handled when the technical framework allows it

labelData.label: If applicable, specify why and for what period a data embargo is needed

fieldSet.title: Specify when the data will be made available for reuse

labelData.label: If the reuse of some data is restricted, explain why

fieldSet.title: Specify whether the data produced and or used in the project is usable by third parties after the end of the project
Specify the length of time for which the data will remain reusable
Do you request how data should be cited when reused?
fieldSet.description: For how to cite data you can consult http://tiny.cc/mf9lcz

fieldSet.title: Are the rights related to the data documented?

options.label: The rights owner is recorded in the metadata form
options.value: The rights owner is recorded in the metadata form

options.label: Documentation is gathered from their holders
options.value: Documentation is gathered from their holders

options.label: Permissions are gathered from their holders
options.value: Permissions are gathered from their holders

options.label: The data creator is responsible for recording any rights
options.value: The data creator is responsible for recording any rights

options.label: If rights are held by third parties, the creator is responsible for ensuring permissions are given, or content removed
options.value: If rights are held by third parties, the creator is responsible for ensuring permissions are given, or content removed

options.label: Provide proper attribution and credit information in an external metadata record where a dataset is implemented by different individual contributors
options.value: Provide proper attribution and credit information in an external metadata record where a dataset is implemented by different individual contributors

fieldSet.title: Describe which information you gather on the rights holder to make sure that nobody is left out

options.label: Set up of scientific and technical committee
options.value: Set up of scientific and technical committee

options.label: Use of tools for automatic checks
options.value: Use of tools for automatic checks

options.label: Consistency verified with data models and standards
options.value: Consistency verified with data models and standards

fieldSet.title: Describe the data quality assurance processes
fieldSet.description: Quality assurance procedures are closely related to RDM and are meant to validate that data are clean (no duplicates or inconsistencies), error-free, well structured and represented in analysis. Quality assurance checks can be facilitated by the use of tools or scripts.
fieldSet.additionalInformation: To familiarise yourself with the process, you may find a checklist here: https://www.eur.nl/sites/corporate/files/2017-11/Checklist_QA_QC_RDM_0_2_MD_20150129.pdf

fieldSet.title: Specify if defined criteria ensuring relevance and understandability of the data for users are available

options.label: Staff with specialized education or training
options.value: Staff with specialized education or training

options.label: Special training course to use specialized infrastructure
options.value: Special training course to use specialized infrastructure

options.label: Domain experts collaborate with technical partners to ensure precise mappings from content providers schemas to project ontology
options.value: Domain experts collaborate with technical partners to ensure precise mappings from content providers schemas to project ontology

fieldSet.title: How do you ensure the availability of sufficient information (technical data and metadata) for end users to enable them to make reliable quality­related evaluations?

section.description: Data can be reused when the conditions about how others can make use of the data are well-described following community-standards and are communicated as specified by the owners. Such information can be found in licenses attributed to data and in references about the data provenance.
section.title: Increase data reuse

fieldSet.title: Estimate the costs for making your data FAIR
fieldSet.description: Research Data Management is not a costless procedure, so it is important that it is thought through at the planning stage of research.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Please indicate the estimation of the total cost for the whole research life cycle (including also cost for archiving and long term preservation)
fieldSet.additionalInformation: To understand what costs are allocated across the steps of a RDM lifecycle, how to estimate them and how to prevent them: https://www.openaire.eu/how-to-comply-to-h2020-mandates-rdm-costs

fieldSet.title: Cost covered by hosting institution
fieldSet.description: Specify which is the "unit" of archiving

fieldSet.title: Describe how you intend to cover these costs
Clearly identify responsibilities for data management in your project
fieldSet.description: Please, list the responsible actors/partners for every data life cycle activity

selectData.label: Indicate the potential value of long term preservation

options.label: Data is potentially important for reuse by a larger community
options.value: Data is potentially important for reuse by a larger community

options.label: Data contributes to improve an open access publication
options.value: Data contributes to improve an open access publication

options.label: Data was produced with a process that is difficult to repeat
options.value: Data was produced with a process that is difficult to repeat

options.label: Data need to be archived because the funder requires it
options.value: Data need to be archived because the funder requires it

fieldSet.title: Describe costs of long term preservation
fieldSet.description: You can get help in calculating Research Data Management (RDM) cost with the Guide available at http://tiny.cc/ob4lcz

section.description: Data management can be costly, especially when its planning hasn't been sufficient from the very beginning of the research process. Costing of data management includes for example potential use of proprietary services and tools or extra effort needed to perform specific tasks or even to develop tools from scratch.
section.title: Allocation of resources

fieldSet.title: Address data recovery as well as secure storage and transfer of sensitive data
Specify if your organization has tools to control the risks associated with receiving, managing, processing and ingesting digital collection content
Specify if you have policies regarding the storage of intermediate results and temporary files
Specify if your system uses automated backup processes, and/or if an automated monitoring process for storage is available

options.label: Assigning Persistent Identifiers (like DOI and URN) to a dataset
options.value: Assigning Persistent Identifiers (like DOI and URN) to a dataset

options.label: Preserving all datastreams in the original format as distinct files
options.value: Preserving all datastreams in the original format as distinct files

options.label: Performing quality checks on submitted metadata
options.value: Performing quality checks on submitted metadata

options.label: Preserving data and metadata via migration
options.value: Preserving data and metadata via migration

fieldSet.title: Describe how the system supports preservation
What tools does your system use to provide access to users?
Describe the digital asset management system used

options.label: Privacy constraints and applicable ethical norms
options.value: Privacy constraints and applicable ethical norms

options.label: Data accompanied by informed consent statements
options.value: Data accompanied by informed consent statements

fieldSet.title: Outline how your project ensures compliance with disciplinary and ethical norms

options.label: My institution has a Research Data Management Protocol
options.value: My institution has a Research Data Management Protocol

options.label: Digital Curation Center Data Management Plan
options.value: Digital Curation Center Data Management Plan

fieldSet.title: Refer to other national/funder/sectorial/departmental procedures for data management that you are using

textListValues.textListValue: Standard Visualizer (e.g. browser, Acrobat Reader, Image Viewer)
Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)
Set up of scientific and technical committee

field.textValue: spring-in, carbon, composites, distortions, monitoring, liquid resin infusion
An article/technical writing regarding the challenges encountered during manufacturing of test specimens through LRI and process monitoring.
Article regarding manufacturing know-how and challenges.

textListValues.textListValue: The rights owner is recorded in the metadata form

field.textValue: Long term preservation is done in-house at INCAS and this costs are covered by the project ever since the preliminary phase.

textListValues.textListValue: Data was produced with a process that is difficult to repeat

field.textValue: INCAS - National Institute for Aerospace Research, Bucharest

textListValues.textListValue: Staff with specialized education or training

field.textValue: Data storage in NAS servers that are constantly backed up by the IT department at INCAS. Data is kept for at least 5 years after project end date.
